input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: Three browned, round pork sausage patties sit diagonally on a white rectangular plate, garnished with fresh green celery leaves. A lower banner features blue text reading "Pork Sausage Patties" and a "how2heroes" logo. The scene cuts to a chef in a white jacket and black-and-white striped apron standing behind a granite counter. Arranged before him are a metal bowl, a container of raw meat, small black cups, liquid containers, and an empty white rectangular plate. Another worker is visible in the background. The chef gestures with his hands, clasping them and opening his palms. The view cuts to a close-up of a metal pan over a blue gas flame. A hand pours liquid into the pan with the text overlay "heat 2 tbsp vegetable oil over medium-low". Finally, chopped white onions are poured from a clear container into the heated oil. From [20-22], a chef wearing a white coat and striped apron stands behind a kitchen counter, initially clasping his hands before extending his left arm to the right to retrieve a small plastic container filled with a pale yellow liquid. The blue text overlay "cook 10 minutes & cool completely" is visible at the bottom. Between [22-24], he lifts

From 112-120s, a chef wearing a white coat and a black-and-white striped apron stands at a granite counter in a commercial kitchen. He is mixing ingredients in a silver metal bowl, initially using both hands and then primarily his right hand while steadying the bowl with his left. To his left on the counter sit a white rectangular plate and a white cloth. To his right are two small black cups and a plastic container holding a yellow liquid. The background features stainless steel counters, sinks, and hanging pendant lights, with the 'how2heroes' logo visible in the top right corner.

[120-128] A chef wearing a white coat and a black apron with white vertical stripes stands at a granite countertop in a commercial kitchen. He is vigorously mixing a meat mixture inside a silver bowl using his right hand while steadying the bowl with his left hand. To his left on the counter sits a white rectangular plate and a folded white cloth. To his right are two small black cups and a plastic container holding a yellow liquid. In the background, stainless steel kitchen equipment and another staff member are visible. The "how2heroes" logo appears in the top right corner. The chef maintains a steady rhythm as he mixes the ingredients thoroughly. The lighting in the kitchen is bright, reflecting off the stainless steel surfaces. The other staff member in the background is also wearing white. The chef looks down at the bowl, concentrating on the task.

[128-130] A chef mixes bowl contents with his right hand while steadying it with his left, then the scene cuts to a close-up of hands kneading a reddish-brown ground meat mixture inside a silver bowl on a granite countertop. Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
